solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public int minSteps(int n) {
        int steps = 0;
        int divisor = 2;
        while (n > 1) {
            while (n % divisor == 0) {
                steps += divisor;
                n /= divisor;
            }
            divisor++;
        }
        return steps;
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(sqrt(n))
space complexity: O(1)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: <The solution uses a factorization approach to determine the minimum number of steps. It iteratively divides the number by its smallest prime factors and accumulates the sum of these factors, which corresponds to the minimum number of operations. This is efficient and avoids unnecessary computations.>
